seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: composite: keep the min-length degree-capped connectivity goal but (a) restrict the output to a per-station tower-capped connected spanning network (caps in {2,3,4}, degree-constrained => NP-hard) and (b) generalize the input with a third altitude coordinate and a vertically-penalised lift cost len = round(horizontal_distance + 3*|delta_h|), turning the plain planar Euclidean MST into an NP-hard degree-constrained MST on an elevation-weighted metric with ridge/basin terrain structure.
title: Ridgeline Lift Grid: Tower-Capped Ski-Lift Network
strategies: chain baseline: link the stations in input order into one lift chain (always feasible, matches the checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)
nearest-neighbour path: start anywhere and repeatedly hop to the cheapest unvisited station under the elevation-weighted cost, forming a short degree-2 chain
degree-constrained Kruskal/Prim: grow a spanning tree by adding globally cheapest lifts whose endpoints still have free tower slots, giving a near-MST that respects the caps
local search: start from a degree-constrained tree and apply cost-reducing edge swaps / branch reroutes that keep degrees within cap
